\documentclass{article}
\begin{document}
Let $f \colon \mathbb{R} \to \mathbb{R}$ be a function.
We have $\sin(x) + \cos(y) = 0$ and $a, b, c$ are integers.
The equation
\[ x = 1 \]
holds, and so does $x = 2$.

\begin{align*}
  a &= b \\
  c &= d.
\end{align*}

For polynomials $f(a,b)$, the parentheses are mathematical.
Use $\dots$ for ellipses.
The dual $V^*$, the norm $|x|$, and $a \bmod n$ are all fine.

\begin{asy}
dot("$A$", A, dir(A));
\end{asy}

\end{document}
